Chordata > Actinopterygii > Beloniformes > > Hemiramphidae

Black-barred halfbeak

Hemiramphus far

Updated Sep 2020

**

Where seen?

This rather plump halfbeak with dark bars on the sides of its body is sometimes seen near reefs.

Features

Those seen about 20cm, can grow to 40cm long. Body long, cylindrical and plump. It has a forked tail, upper lobe of the tail is yellowish and shorter than the lower lobe. 3 to 9 short black bars on the side of the body. Lower jaw elongated but not very long compared to the body. The tip of the lower jaw may be reddish. It breeds in estuaries.

Sultan Shoal, Jan 11
Tanah Merah, Dec 09

What does it eat? The adults feed on seagrasses and to a lesser extent, green seaweeds and diatoms.

Human uses: Considered tasty, it is marketed fresh and dried.

Black-barred halfbeaks on Singapore shores

On wildsingapore flickr

  • Photos for free download
  • Map of sightings in Singapore

Other sightings on Singapore shores

Pulau Semakau (South), Nov 24
Photo shared by Loh Kok Sheng on facebook .
Beting Bemban Besar, Oct 25 Photo shared by Lon Voon Ong on facebook .
